data.content:
# Implementation Plan — Host agent-friendly API resource (foolery-ddb9)

## Goal recap
Make Foolery's HTTP API discoverable and machine-consumable so an agent can,
from a clean shell, find the OpenAPI spec, resolve a repo by name, and call a
documented endpoint without reading source. All changes ADDITIVE — keep
`/api/openapi.json` backward compatible, no route-breaking changes, no PR workflow.

## Current state (researched)
- Spec is hand-written, modular: `src/lib/openapi-spec.ts` assembles path modules
  from `src/lib/openapi/paths-*.ts` + schemas from `src/lib/openapi/schemas*.ts`.
  Served by `src/app/api/openapi.json/route.ts` (OpenAPI 3.1.0).
- Already present: 13 tags, operationIds on all ops, modular component schemas,
  `_repo` query param convention, registry route. Examples are RICH in plans only,
  SPARSE elsewhere. `servers: [{url:"/"}]`. `info.description` is one terse line.
- Docs at `src/app/api/docs/route.ts` = bare ReDoc loading `/api/openapi.json`.
  ReDoc renders `info.description` markdown + tag descriptions prominently.
- Repo resolution: `GET /api/registry` -> `{data:[{path,name,addedAt,memoryManagerType}]}`.
  Agents resolve by `name` (basename) then pass `path` as the `_repo` query param.
  Schema: `RegisteredRepo` in `src/lib/openapi/schemas.ts:290`.
- Envelopes: `{data:T}` (most), `{ok:true,data:T}` (settings/doctor/version),
  `{error,banner?,marker?}` (errors). Status mapping in `src/lib/backend-http.ts`.
- NO `/.well-known/` or discovery endpoint exists today.

## Approach: 4 additive layers
### A. Enrich the OpenAPI spec metadata (machine discovery)
- NEW `src/lib/openapi/agent-guide.ts` — single source for agent-facing prose +
  the discovery document, imported by both the spec and the well-known route
  (avoids duplication; keeps `openapi-spec.ts` small / under 500 lines):
  - `export const agentGuideMarkdown` — onboarding narrative covering: local base
    URL discovery (dev :3000, installed runtime :3210, relative `/` default),
    repo resolution flow (GET /api/registry -> match by `name` -> use `path` as
    `_repo`), response envelope conventions (`{data}` / `{ok,data}` / `{error,
    banner,marker}`), error->HTTP status mapping, and ONE complete worked example
    (find spec -> list registry -> resolve repo by name -> GET /api/beats?_repo=…).
  - `export const discoveryDocument` — small JSON: `{name, version, openapi:
    "/api/openapi.json", docs:"/api/docs", registry:"/api/registry",
    capabilities:"/api/capabilities", version:"/api/version", conventions:{…},
    quickstart:[…steps…]}`.
  - If markdown + doc push the file over 500 lines, split markdown into
    `agent-guide-markdown.ts`.
- EDIT `src/lib/openapi-spec.ts` (additive only):
  - Set `info.description` to the existing summary PLUS `agentGuideMarkdown`
    (ReDoc renders it at the top of the docs page — satisfies AC2 in-spec).
  - Add `externalDocs: {description, url:"/api/docs"}`.
  - Expand `servers` keeping `{url:"/"}` FIRST (preserves relative-URL clients):
    add `http://localhost:3000` (dev) and `http://localhost:3210` (installed) with
    descriptions.
  - Add vendor extension `x-agent-discovery: "/.well-known/foolery.json"`.
  - Add reusable `components.parameters.RepoParam` for `_repo` (path-based repo
    selector) and reference it where helpful (non-breaking; existing inline params
    can stay).

### B. Make /api/docs an agent onboarding page
- Primary mechanism is (A): the enriched `info.description` markdown renders in
  ReDoc, giving base URL, registry resolution, envelopes, and a full workflow
  example on the human-readable page (AC2).
- EDIT `src/app/api/docs/route.ts` (minimal): add a small top banner/link to
  `/.well-known/foolery.json` and `/api/openapi.json` so humans see the machine
  entrypoint. Keep ReDoc + CDN load intact.

### C. Stable machine-discovery entrypoint (well-known)
- NEW `src/app/.well-known/foolery.json/route.ts` — GET returns `discoveryDocument`
  from agent-guide; cacheable. Idiomatic RFC 8615 root entrypoint linking spec,
  docs, registry, capabilities, version + quickstart.
- Verify the `.well-known` path is reachable under Next app router (no middleware
  rewrite intercepts it). FALLBACK if problematic: also/instead serve
  `/api/discovery` with the same document.

### D. Examples + verification
- Add `examples` to sparse high-value ops (watch 500-line / 100-col limits;
  introduce a shared `paths-examples.ts` if a module gets close):
  registry GET (`paths-system.ts`), settings GET (`paths-settings.ts`),
  terminal create (`paths-streaming.ts`), core beats list/create (`paths-beats.ts`),
  stale-grooming list (`paths-stale-grooming.ts`).
- NEW hermetic test `src/lib/__tests__/openapi-discovery.test.ts` (no env/fs/net):
  import `openApiSpec` + `discoveryDocument` and assert:
  - paths exist for stale grooming, registry, settings, terminal, plans, core beats;
  - every operation has `operationId` + `tags`;
  - `info.description` mentions base URL / registry / envelope (agent guidance);
  - `discoveryDocument` references `/api/openapi.json`, `/api/docs`, `/api/registry`;
  - `components.parameters.RepoParam` exists.
- NEW manual smoke (live server, non-CI) under `src/app/api/__manual_tests__/` OR
  `scripts/agent-discovery-smoke.sh`: curl `/.well-known/foolery.json` ->
  `/api/openapi.json` -> `/api/registry` (resolve repo by name) -> one documented
  endpoint with `_repo`. This is the repeatable AC3 discovery flow.
- DOC pointer: short `docs/API.md` (or section in `docs/DEVELOPING.md`) describing
  the discovery entrypoint + smoke script.

## Files
- NEW: `src/lib/openapi/agent-guide.ts` (+ optional `agent-guide-markdown.ts`)
- NEW: `src/app/.well-known/foolery.json/route.ts`
- NEW: `src/lib/__tests__/openapi-discovery.test.ts`
- NEW: `scripts/agent-discovery-smoke.sh` (or `__manual_tests__` equivalent)
- NEW: `docs/API.md` (or edit `docs/DEVELOPING.md`)
- EDIT: `src/lib/openapi-spec.ts`, `src/app/api/docs/route.ts`,
  `paths-system.ts`, `paths-settings.ts`, `paths-streaming.ts`,
  `paths-beats.ts`, `paths-stale-grooming.ts` (examples)

## Test strategy
- Hermetic unit test asserts on the STATIC spec object + discovery document
  (satisfies Hermetic Test Policy — no process.env, fs, network, host binaries).
- Manual smoke script exercises the live curl discovery flow (AC3, AC4) and is
  excluded from CI by policy.
- Before completing implementation: `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`,
  `bun run test`, `bun run build`.

## Risks & mitigations
- ESLint `max-lines`(500)/`max-len`(100): split agent-guide markdown; shared
  examples module; wrap long example strings.
- `.well-known` may be intercepted by Next middleware/rewrites -> verify; fallback
  `/api/discovery` alias.
- ReDoc renders markdown only (no scripts) -> keep guidance as markdown.
- CLAUDE.md "State Classification Is Loom-Derived": example prose MUST NOT bake
  `ready_for_*` or specific state names as contract — keep examples state-neutral
  or point at `GET /api/workflows` for live states.
- Backward compat: `{url:"/"}` stays first in `servers`; all spec additions are
  additive; existing operationIds/paths untouched.

## Complexity
Medium. ~8 files, predominantly additive content (metadata, markdown, one new
route, tests, docs). Low architectural risk; no backend or workflow changes.

## Child knots
None. This is one cohesive, additive deliverable; decomposition would add
overhead without parallelism benefit.
